DISCOVERY 302, MOTION OF DEFENDANT HEIDI DETJEN TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES, TO HAVE MATTERS DEEMED ADMITTED, AND MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AND WAS UNOPPOSED. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE TWENTY DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THE SERVICE OF THE ORDER HEREIN WITH NOTICE OF ENTRY TO RESPOND TO SAID WRITTEN DISCOVERY. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AS AGAINST THE PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $750.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN THE AFORESAID TWENTY DAYS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-21-2016 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by Plaintiff. Mark Dawson, Esq. appearing for Defendant.

The Court having reviewed the file finds no good cause or substantial justification for plaintiff's failure to comply with the Court's orders and rules. Therefore, it is hereby ordered that this matter be dismissed pursuant to Government Code 68608(b). The Court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance.

JUDGE SUZANNE R. BOLANOS; Clerk MOlopernes/YMazariegos. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HEIDI DETJEN Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, To Have Matters Deemed Admitted, And Monetary Sanctions is granted and was unopposed. Motion For Hearing De Novo Or Reconsideration In Opposition To Deft'S Motion To Strike Plntf'S
Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PATRICIA FRANTOM'S MOTION FOR HEARING DE NOVO OR RECONSIDERATION IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE is DENIED. The motion is untimely under CCP 1008(a) and improper under CCP 1008(b); the notice of motion is insufficient under CCP 1005 and 1013. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B.
